title: Spergularia macrorrhiza
taxonomy: Spergularia macrorrhiza is classified within the family Caryophyllaceae, commonly known as the pink or carnation family. This classification places it among the flowering plants and more specifically within the order Caryophyllales. The family Caryophyllaceae is a diverse group of plants that includes several species of annuals and perennials. Spergularia macrorrhiza, a perennial species, is native to the southwestern parts of Europe and North Africa. Its classification provides insight into its evolutionary relationships with other species within the family.

intro: Spergularia macrorrhiza, also known as coastal sand-spurrey, is a perennial plant species belonging to the Caryophyllaceae family. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health conditions, including respiratory issues, digestive problems, and skin infections, due to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal properties. The bioactive constituents of Spergularia macrorrhiza include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Spergularia macrorrhiza is often used to prepare teas, infusions, and tinctures to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs, and to soothe skin irritations and wounds.
